truthAudit.Summary: Run 2 identified 8 of the 20 true vulnerabilities. It correctly quoted evidence for format string injection, use-after-free, command injection (config), stack buffer overflow, heap buffer overflow, hardcoded credentials, and division by zero. It missed 12 vulnerabilities including integer overflow, race conditions, logic bombs, path traversal, weak PRNG, thread-unsafe caching, insecure temp files, persistent auth state, unhandled exceptions, integer underflow, unbounded recursion, and data races. Several severity and CWE classifications were overclaimed or mismatched.

Items.Notes: Run 2 explicitly identified the format string vulnerability in the log function and quoted the exact printf call using the user-controlled format string.
Run 2 did not mention integer overflow in the factorial computation loop (result *= i).
Run 2 correctly identified the use-after-free risk in deferred history flush, noting the raw pointer capture and subsequent pool cleanup.
Run 2 explicitly flagged command injection in config validation, quoting the system() call constructed from config settings.
Run 2 correctly identified the stack buffer overflow in evaluate_expression, quoting the unchecked strcpy into a fixed-size stack buffer.
Run 2 did not identify the race condition in the thread-pool counter (volatile read before lock, stale old_value assignment).
Run 2 did not identify the logic bomb bypass in authentication (EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE after >5 attempts).
Run 2 did not identify path traversal in configuration loading (unvalidated filename concatenation).
Run 2 correctly identified the heap overflow in the expression parser, quoting the unchecked strcpy into a 256-byte pool-allocated buffer.
Run 2 explicitly identified hardcoded administrative credentials and quoted the exact constexpr definition.
Run 2 did not identify the weak PRNG usage for session tokens (std::mt19937 seeded with std::random_device but small range and predictable output).
Run 2 did not identify the thread-unsafe static tm cache in logging (localtime returns a pointer to static data overwritten by other threads).
Run 2 did not identify insecure temporary file handling (system command appending to temp dir log without atomicity or restrictions).
Run 2 did not identify persistent authentication state across sessions or lack of session invalidation.
Run 2 did not identify unhandled exceptions during variable parsing (std::stod on tokens[3] without try-catch in handle_variable_command).
Run 2 did not identify integer underflow in memory-pool block split (previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES can wrap if previous_size is small).
Run 2 did not identify unbounded recursion in the parser (parse_expression -> parse_factor -> PAREN_OPEN -> parse_expression without depth limiting).
Run 2 did not identify the data race in the result cache (result_cache_ accessed/modified without cache_mutex_ protection during thread_pool_->enqueue).
Run 2 did not identify TOCTOU in config loading (std::filesystem::exists check followed by sleep_for and std::ifstream open).
Run 2 correctly identified division by zero in route score calculation, quoting the exact expression dividing by token_length without zero-check.
